words.definition: The period shortly after sunrise or before sunset when daylight is soft, warm and golden.
words.example: Photographers love shooting portraits during the golden hour.

words.definition: A streak of light in the sky produced by a small piece of rock burning as it enters the atmosphere.
words.example: A bright meteor flashed across the sky.

words.definition: Puffy, cotton-like clouds with flat bases and rounded tops, common in fair weather.
words.example: Cumulus clouds drifted lazily overhead.

words.definition: A natural coloured light display in the sky, seen mainly near the poles.
words.example: We travelled to Norway to see the aurora.

words.definition: An arc of colours in the sky caused by sunlight refracting through water droplets.
words.example: A rainbow appeared after the shower.

words.definition: The pleasant earthy smell produced when rain falls on dry soil.
words.example: The garden filled with the scent of petrichor.

words.definition: A period of calm, happy weather traditionally around the winter solstice.
words.example: Sailors welcomed the halcyon days.

words.definition: A soft, gentle breeze, especially from the west.
words.example: A cool zephyr rustled the leaves.

words.definition: An optical illusion caused by heat, often making distant objects look like water.
words.example: In the desert we saw a shimmering mirage.

words.definition: An event in which one heavenly body is obscured by another, such as the sun by the moon.
words.example: A total eclipse darkened the town at noon.

words.definition: The time when the sun reaches its highest or lowest point in the sky at noon.
words.example: The summer solstice is the longest day of the year.

words.definition: One of two days each year when day and night are about equal in length.
words.example: Spring begins near the March equinox.

words.definition: A very large sea wave caused by an underwater earthquake or eruption.
words.example: The tsunami swept across the coast.

words.definition: A bright spot in the sky beside the sun caused by ice crystals refracting light.
words.example: Two sundogs glowed on either side of the low sun.

words.definition: A ring of light around the sun or moon caused by ice crystals in high clouds.
words.example: A pale halo circled the winter moon.

words.definition: A dark, dense cloud that produces rain or snow.
words.example: The nimbus overhead promised a downpour.

words.definition: Thin, wispy clouds of ice crystals found high in the sky.
words.example: Delicate cirrus streaks lined the horizon.

words.definition: Low, grey, sheet-like clouds that often bring drizzle.
words.example: Stratus covered the whole valley.

words.definition: A pale, colourless arc similar to a rainbow, formed in fog.
words.example: A ghostly fogbow appeared over the moor.

words.definition: A rainbow produced by moonlight rather than sunlight.
words.example: We glimpsed a faint moonbow at the waterfall.

words.definition: A rosy glow seen on mountain peaks at sunrise or sunset.
words.example: Alpenglow lit the snowy summit.

words.definition: Sunbeams that appear to radiate from a single point in the sky.
words.example: Crepuscular rays streamed through the clouds.

words.definition: The natural production of light by living organisms.
words.example: The bay glowed at night from bioluminescence.

words.definition: A soft glow emitted by a substance without noticeable heat.
words.example: The dial showed a green phosphorescence.

words.definition: A glassy tube formed in sand or rock when lightning strikes.
words.example: They dug up a slender fulgurite from the dune.

words.definition: A warm, dry wind that descends the lee side of a mountain range.
words.example: A foehn melted the alpine snow rapidly.

words.definition: A hot, dusty wind blowing from North Africa across the Mediterranean.
words.example: The sirocco tinted the sky orange.

words.definition: A warm, dry wind that blows down the eastern slopes of the Rocky Mountains.
words.example: A chinook raised the temperature by twenty degrees.

words.definition: A seasonal wind bringing heavy rains to South and Southeast Asia.
words.example: The monsoon flooded the streets.

words.definition: A tropical cyclone occurring in the northwestern Pacific Ocean.
words.example: A powerful typhoon struck the coast.

words.definition: A severe tropical cyclone in the Atlantic or eastern Pacific.
words.example: The hurricane made landfall overnight.

words.definition: A large weather system with rotating winds around a low-pressure centre.
words.example: A cyclone battered the fishing fleet.

words.definition: A violently rotating column of air extending from a thunderstorm to the ground.
words.example: A tornado tore through the plains.

words.definition: A rotating column of water and spray forming over a lake or sea.
words.example: A waterspout danced across the bay.

words.definition: A small, brief whirlwind that lifts dust from the ground.
words.example: A dust devil skittered across the field.

words.definition: A dense sandstorm carried by a wall of wind.
words.example: A haboob engulfed the desert town.

words.definition: A widespread, long-lived, straight-line windstorm from thunderstorms.
words.example: A derecho knocked out power across the state.

words.definition: A powerful downdraft from a thunderstorm affecting a small area.
words.example: The microburst flipped several planes on the tarmac.

words.definition: Streaks of rain or snow that evaporate before reaching the ground.
words.example: Grey virga hung beneath the cloud.

words.definition: Pouch-like formations hanging beneath thunderstorm clouds.
words.example: Mammatus dotted the sky after the storm.

words.definition: A lens-shaped cloud that forms over mountains in strong winds.
words.example: A giant lenticular cloud hovered above the peak.

words.definition: Rare, iridescent polar clouds seen at high altitudes.
words.example: Nacreous clouds gleamed pink and green at dusk.

words.definition: Thin, glowing clouds seen in twilight at very high altitudes.
words.example: Noctilucent clouds shimmered above the horizon.

words.definition: A rare luminous sphere seen during thunderstorms.
words.example: Witnesses claim ball lightning entered the room.

words.definition: A bluish glow around pointed objects during a thunderstorm.
words.example: St. Elmo's fire flickered on the ship's mast.

words.definition: A brief red flash of light high above thunderstorms.
words.example: Cameras captured a sprite over the storm cell.

words.definition: The soft light in the sky after sunset or before sunrise.
words.example: We walked home in the twilight.

words.definition: The first appearance of light in the sky before sunrise.
words.example: Birds began to sing at dawn.

words.definition: The darker part of twilight, especially in the evening.
words.example: Bats emerged at dusk.

words.definition: Twilight, especially at evening; poetic dusk.
words.example: She sat on the porch in the gloaming.

words.definition: The point in the sky directly above an observer.
words.example: The sun stood near its zenith at midday.

words.definition: The point of the sky directly below an observer, opposite the zenith.
words.example: The stars near the nadir were unseen.

words.definition: The point in an orbit closest to the sun.
words.example: Earth reaches perihelion in early January.

words.definition: The point in an orbit farthest from the sun.
words.example: Earth reaches aphelion in early July.

words.definition: A straight-line configuration of three celestial bodies.
words.example: A syzygy causes eclipses and spring tides.

words.definition: The hiding of one celestial body by another passing in front of it.
words.example: Astronomers observed the occultation of the star.

words.definition: An event in which many meteors appear to come from one point in the sky.
words.example: The Perseid meteor shower peaks each August.

words.definition: An exceptionally bright meteor that may explode in the atmosphere.
words.example: A bolide lit the night sky like day.

words.definition: An icy body that develops a glowing tail as it nears the sun.
words.example: Halley's comet returns every 76 years.

words.definition: A small rocky body orbiting the sun, mainly between Mars and Jupiter.
words.example: A large asteroid passed near Earth.

words.definition: A cloud of gas and dust in outer space.
words.example: The Orion Nebula is visible with binoculars.

words.definition: A powerful stellar explosion at the end of a star's life.
words.example: A supernova outshone the whole galaxy.

words.definition: The galaxy that contains our solar system, seen as a band of light in the sky.
words.example: On clear nights the Milky Way stretches overhead.

words.definition: A vast system of stars, gas and dust held together by gravity.
words.example: The Andromeda galaxy is our nearest neighbour.

words.definition: A group of stars forming a recognisable pattern.
words.example: Orion is a winter constellation.

words.definition: The apparent path of the sun across the sky over the year.
words.example: Planets travel close to the ecliptic.

words.definition: A belt of the sky containing the paths of the sun, moon and main planets.
words.example: The zodiac is divided into twelve signs.

words.definition: The lighter outer part of a shadow, as during an eclipse.
words.example: The moon crossed Earth's penumbra.

words.definition: The darkest, innermost part of a shadow.
words.example: At totality the moon lies inside the umbra.

words.definition: A brief flash of sunlight seen at the start or end of a total solar eclipse.
words.example: The crowd gasped at the diamond ring effect.

words.definition: A temporary dark patch on the sun's surface caused by magnetic activity.
words.example: A large sunspot appeared this week.

words.definition: A sudden burst of radiation from the sun's surface.
words.example: A solar flare disrupted radio signals.

words.definition: A stream of charged particles flowing from the sun into space.
words.example: The solar wind shapes comet tails.

words.definition: A region of the stratosphere containing much of the atmosphere's ozone.
words.example: The ozone layer protects us from UV rays.

words.definition: The layer of the atmosphere containing charged particles that reflect radio waves.
words.example: Short-wave radio bounces off the ionosphere.

words.definition: The atmospheric layer above the troposphere, up to about 50 km.
words.example: Ozone concentrates in the stratosphere.

words.definition: The lowest layer of Earth's atmosphere, where weather occurs.
words.example: Nearly all clouds form in the troposphere.

words.definition: A narrow, fast air current high in the atmosphere.
words.example: A strong jet stream shortened the flight.

words.definition: Steady tropical winds blowing toward the equator.
words.example: The ship rode the trade winds westward.

words.definition: A region near the equator with light, variable winds.
words.example: Sailing ships once dreaded the doldrums.

words.definition: A periodic warming of the tropical Pacific that alters weather worldwide.
words.example: El Niño brought droughts to Australia.

words.definition: A periodic cooling of the tropical Pacific with opposite effects to El Niño.
words.example: La Niña strengthened Atlantic hurricanes.

words.definition: A powerful warm ocean current in the north Atlantic.
words.example: The Gulf Stream keeps Britain mild.

words.definition: The rising of deep, cold, nutrient-rich water toward the surface.
words.example: Upwelling supports rich fisheries off Peru.

words.definition: A layer in a body of water where temperature changes rapidly with depth.
words.example: Divers noticed the chill below the thermocline.

words.definition: A play of colours produced by reflection, as on soap bubbles or oil films.
words.example: The cloud showed a delicate iridescence.

words.definition: A white deposit of ice formed on cold surfaces from freezing fog.
words.example: Rime coated every branch of the trees.

words.definition: A greyish-white crystalline deposit of frozen water vapour on cold surfaces.
words.example: Hoarfrost sparkled on the morning grass.

words.definition: A severe snowstorm with strong winds and low visibility.
words.example: A blizzard closed the mountain pass.

words.definition: A sudden, violent gust of wind, often with rain or snow.
words.example: A squall swept across the lake.

words.definition: Precipitation of partly frozen rain or a mixture of rain and snow.
words.example: Icy sleet stung our faces.

words.definition: Soft pellets of snow-covered ice that fall from clouds.
words.example: Graupel bounced off the roof.

words.definition: Compacted, granular snow that is on its way to becoming glacier ice.
words.example: Layers of firn made up the glacier.

words.definition: A large mass of snow, ice or rock that slides swiftly down a mountain.
words.example: An avalanche blocked the road.

words.definition: The sliding down of a mass of earth or rock from a mountain or cliff.
words.example: Heavy rain triggered a landslide.

words.definition: A hot spring that periodically shoots up jets of water and steam.
words.example: Old Faithful is a famous geyser.

words.definition: An opening in the earth's crust that emits steam and gases.
words.example: Steam rose from a hillside fumarole.

words.definition: A large volcanic crater formed by the collapse of the volcano's mouth.
words.example: A lake filled the ancient caldera.

words.definition: A standing wave oscillating in an enclosed body of water.
words.example: A seiche rocked boats on the lake.

words.definition: A large tidal wave that travels up a river against the current.
words.example: Surfers rode the tidal bore upstream.

quiz.explanation: Gulf Stream (noun) — A powerful warm ocean current in the north Atlantic.

quiz.question: Which word means: "The rising of deep, cold, nutrient-rich water toward the surface."?
quiz.explanation: Upwelling (noun) — The rising of deep, cold, nutrient-rich water toward the surface.

quiz.question: Which word means: "A layer in a body of water where temperature changes rapidly with depth."?
quiz.explanation: Thermocline (noun) — A layer in a body of water where temperature changes rapidly with depth.

quiz.question: Which word means: "A play of colours produced by reflection, as on soap bubbles or oil films."?
quiz.explanation: Iridescence (noun) — A play of colours produced by reflection, as on soap bubbles or oil films.

quiz.question: Which word means: "A white deposit of ice formed on cold surfaces from freezing fog."?
quiz.explanation: Rime (noun) — A white deposit of ice formed on cold surfaces from freezing fog.

quiz.question: Which word means: "A greyish-white crystalline deposit of frozen water vapour on cold surfaces."?
quiz.explanation: Hoarfrost (noun) — A greyish-white crystalline deposit of frozen water vapour on cold surfaces.

quiz.question: Which word means: "A severe snowstorm with strong winds and low visibility."?
quiz.explanation: Blizzard (noun) — A severe snowstorm with strong winds and low visibility.

quiz.question: Which word means: "A sudden, violent gust of wind, often with rain or snow."?
quiz.explanation: Squall (noun) — A sudden, violent gust of wind, often with rain or snow.

quiz.question: Which word means: "Precipitation of partly frozen rain or a mixture of rain and snow."?
quiz.explanation: Sleet (noun) — Precipitation of partly frozen rain or a mixture of rain and snow.

quiz.question: Which word means: "Soft pellets of snow-covered ice that fall from clouds."?
quiz.explanation: Graupel (noun) — Soft pellets of snow-covered ice that fall from clouds.

quiz.question: Which word means: "Compacted, granular snow that is on its way to becoming glacier ice."?
quiz.explanation: Firn (noun) — Compacted, granular snow that is on its way to becoming glacier ice.

quiz.question: Which word means: "A large mass of snow, ice or rock that slides swiftly down a mountain."?
quiz.explanation: Avalanche (noun) — A large mass of snow, ice or rock that slides swiftly down a mountain.

quiz.question: Which word means: "The sliding down of a mass of earth or rock from a mountain or cliff."?
quiz.explanation: Landslide (noun) — The sliding down of a mass of earth or rock from a mountain or cliff.

quiz.question: Which word means: "A hot spring that periodically shoots up jets of water and steam."?
quiz.explanation: Geyser (noun) — A hot spring that periodically shoots up jets of water and steam.

quiz.question: Which word means: "An opening in the earth's crust that emits steam and gases."?
quiz.explanation: Fumarole (noun) — An opening in the earth's crust that emits steam and gases.

quiz.question: Which word means: "A large volcanic crater formed by the collapse of the volcano's mouth."?
quiz.explanation: Caldera (noun) — A large volcanic crater formed by the collapse of the volcano's mouth.

quiz.question: Which word means: "A standing wave oscillating in an enclosed body of water."?
quiz.explanation: Seiche (noun) — A standing wave oscillating in an enclosed body of water.

quiz.question: Which word means: "A large tidal wave that travels up a river against the current."?
quiz.explanation: Bore (noun) — A large tidal wave that travels up a river against the current.
